tasks.context: Frozen Stage 1 question: does the technically valid, accessible and immutable Stage 1 build produce voluntary replay and improvement behavior strongly enough to justify Stage 2?
tasks.title: Freeze the Stage 1 candidate build and evidence method
tasks.summary: Select one immutable candidate build, tuning profile and versioned developer-gate method.
tasks.objective: Create the auditable gate baseline without changing implementation, thresholds or accepted evidence.
inputs: Consumed contracts: BuildIdentity/ContentVersion v1 and Diagnostics/Run Validity v1; immutable manifests, evidence protocol, weapon/enemy human gates and AR-012 readiness report.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: all implementation, tuning, scenes, prefabs, registries, source evidence, planning thresholds and task artifacts outside owned gate records.
Serialized ownership: none. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: none.
Rollback: revoke/remove only the freeze records and return the gate to blocked. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: The exact commit, build/content/tuning identity, checksums and gate method are frozen and mutually consistent.
All AR-012 blockers are resolved or the freeze is refused.
Thresholds and invalid-session rules match the accepted planning package before sessions begin.
verification: Automated: validate record shape, fingerprints, checksums, required evidence links and threshold version.
Manual: human lead launches the candidate and cross-checks representative evidence without modifying it.
tasks.handoff_notes: Any candidate change invalidates later gate evidence and requires a new versioned freeze.
tasks.notes: Milestone-cap spend: 0.20 focused lead day allocated to S1.4.

tasks.context: Frozen Stage 1 question: does the technically valid, accessible and immutable Stage 1 build produce voluntary replay and improvement behavior strongly enough to justify Stage 2?
tasks.title: Execute ten consecutive technically valid developer sessions
tasks.summary: Run the accepted candidate through ten consecutive complete developer sessions.
tasks.objective: Prove technical validity before behavioral replay evidence is collected.
inputs: Consumed contracts: Diagnostics/Run Validity v1 and BuildIdentity/ContentVersion v1; frozen candidate, repeated-session, validity, performance, clean-build and accessibility evidence.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: implementation, tuning, candidate build, source evidence, thresholds and external-round records.
Serialized ownership: none. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: none.
Rollback: invalidate the run set and start a fresh versioned sequence; never edit completed sessions. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: Ten consecutive sessions complete without crash, progression blocker, broken restart, unusable controls or lost settings.
Every session links immutable identity, loadout, restarts, deaths, completion and invalidating commands.
A technical failure breaks the sequence and is recorded as reliability evidence rather than a gameplay result.
verification: Automated: validate sequence length/order, identity consistency, completion, diagnostic links and zero invalid sessions.
Manual/playable: human lead completes all ten documented arena/route sessions on the candidate build.
tasks.handoff_notes: GATE-003 starts only after a valid ten-session sequence exists.
tasks.notes: Milestone-cap spend: 0.60 focused lead day allocated to S1.4.

tasks.context: Frozen Stage 1 question: does the technically valid, accessible and immutable Stage 1 build produce voluntary replay and improvement behavior strongly enough to justify Stage 2?
tasks.title: Execute the developer voluntary-replay gate
tasks.summary: Record voluntary replay and mastery behavior after fix-verification play has ended.
tasks.objective: Determine whether the human lead chooses to replay for enjoyment or improvement across three separate days.
inputs: Consumed contracts: BuildIdentity/ContentVersion v1 and Diagnostics/Run Validity v1; technically valid candidate and accepted developer behavioral thresholds.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: implementation, tuning, candidate records, technical-session evidence, thresholds and external-round files.
Serialized ownership: none. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: none.
Rollback: invalidate only contaminated sessions and repeat under a newly documented window; never manufacture voluntariness. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: At least six genuinely voluntary additional runs occur across three separate days using at least two loadouts.
At least one run explicitly pursues better route, execution, time, score or challenge.
Recurring frustration and avoidance are recorded honestly; technically invalid sessions are excluded and linked.
verification: Automated: validate dates, count, loadout diversity, improvement intent, identity and validity links.
Manual/playable: human lead records contemporaneous motivation before each optional run and a bounded post-run note.
edge_cases: run motivated by testing a fix
tasks.handoff_notes: Fix-verification runs do not count; GATE-004 independently reviews this evidence.
tasks.notes: Milestone-cap spend: 0.90 focused lead day allocated to S1.4 across the three-day observation window.

tasks.context: Frozen Stage 1 question: does the technically valid, accessible and immutable Stage 1 build produce voluntary replay and improvement behavior strongly enough to justify Stage 2?
tasks.title: Record the developer-gate decision
tasks.summary: Review technical and voluntary-play evidence and decide whether external testing may begin.
tasks.objective: Create an explicit pass/block decision without silently waiving failed criteria.
inputs: Consumed contracts: Diagnostics/Run Validity v1 and BuildIdentity/ContentVersion v1; AR-012 readiness, GATE-002 technical evidence and GATE-003 behavioral evidence.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: implementation, evidence sources, thresholds, candidate build, external-round records and Stage 2 unlock.
Serialized ownership: none. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: none.
Rollback: revoke the decision and return external testing to blocked. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: Decision addresses every technical and behavioral criterion with direct evidence links.
Only a PASS may complete this task and unblock GATE-005; a block/fail record leaves it blocked or rejected.
No failed accessibility, reliability, performance, feel or replay criterion is silently waived.
verification: Automated: validate decision schema, signatures, fingerprints, evidence links and blocker propagation.
Manual: human lead performs an explicit go/no-go review and records rationale/date.
tasks.handoff_notes: A non-pass prevents downstream dependencies; do not mark this task done merely because a failure report exists.
tasks.notes: Milestone-cap spend: 0.20 focused lead day allocated to S1.4. Developer-gate allocation totals 1.90 days, leaving 0.20 S1.4 day for review reserve.

tasks.context: Frozen Stage 1 question: does the technically valid, accessible and immutable Stage 1 build produce voluntary replay and improvement behavior strongly enough to justify Stage 2?
tasks.title: Freeze the external formal-round protocol and participants
tasks.summary: Version the immutable external method, candidate build, target-player criteria and participant roster.
tasks.objective: Prevent post-hoc threshold, population or method changes before external evidence collection.
inputs: Consumed contracts: BuildIdentity/ContentVersion v1 and Diagnostics/Run Validity v1; evidence protocol, immutable readiness package, passed developer gate and accepted 6-10-player thresholds.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: implementation, thresholds, source evidence, candidate build, participant private data, results and Stage 2 records.
Serialized ownership: none. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: none.
Rollback: cancel the round and revoke roster/protocol before sessions; never rewrite after collection starts. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: Protocol freezes 6-10 qualified target players, exclusion criteria, same build/method and accepted pass thresholds.
Roster excludes close collaborators primarily motivated by helping and stores no unnecessary personal data.
Behavioral measures, interviews, invalid-session rules and seven-day follow-up are fixed before session one.
verification: Automated: validate population bounds, pseudonymous IDs, threshold version, build fingerprint and required method fields.
Manual: human lead reviews consent/privacy handling, recruitment fit and immutable method.
tasks.handoff_notes: Any build/method change versions and restarts the round.
tasks.notes: Milestone-cap spend: 0.30 focused lead day allocated to S1.5.

tasks.context: Frozen Stage 1 question: does the technically valid, accessible and immutable Stage 1 build produce voluntary replay and improvement behavior strongly enough to justify Stage 2?
tasks.title: Conduct the external Stage 1 sessions
tasks.summary: Run the frozen first activity, optional replay opportunity, interviews and follow-up for every participant.
tasks.objective: Collect immutable behavioral and explanatory evidence without coaching, build changes or post-hoc exclusions.
inputs: Consumed contracts: BuildIdentity/ContentVersion v1 and Diagnostics/Run Validity v1; frozen protocol, participant roster and candidate artifact.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: implementation, candidate build, protocol/roster, thresholds, analysis, raw identifying data and Stage 2 records.
Serialized ownership: none. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: none.
Rollback: session evidence is immutable; contamination invalidates and reruns only according to protocol. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: Every scheduled participant receives the same frozen build, instructions, optional replay opportunity and evidence rules.
Behavioral actions are recorded before interviews explain them, including meaningful reruns, loadout/route changes and improvement attempts.
Technical invalidity, withdrawals, discomfort and deviations are retained and classified, never silently excluded.
verification: Automated: validate roster/session coverage, identity, protocol version, required events, follow-up window and immutable checksums.
Manual/playable: facilitator follows the frozen script and records deviations contemporaneously.
tasks.handoff_notes: GATE-007 and GATE-008 consume the immutable session index independently.
tasks.notes: Milestone-cap spend: 1.80 focused lead days allocated to S1.5.

tasks.context: Frozen Stage 1 question: does the technically valid, accessible and immutable Stage 1 build produce voluntary replay and improvement behavior strongly enough to justify Stage 2?
tasks.title: Analyze external behavioral results and interviews
tasks.summary: Compute the frozen behavioral thresholds and use interviews only to explain observed behavior.
tasks.objective: Produce reproducible pass/fail calculations without changing population, denominators or criteria.
inputs: Consumed contracts: Diagnostics/Run Validity v1; frozen protocol and immutable session index/behavior/interview records.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: source sessions, protocol, thresholds, implementation, candidate build, validity audit and final decision.
Serialized ownership: none. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: none.
Rollback: remove derived analysis and regenerate from immutable sessions. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: Calculations report meaningful rerun/loadout-trial, additional-run/request and variation/improvement rates against 60/40/50 percent thresholds.
Denominators and exclusions follow the frozen protocol; interviews explain rather than override behavior.
Recurring readability, control or frustration issues affecting a majority are explicitly surfaced.
verification: Automated: recompute metrics from session index and validate denominators, rounding, thresholds and source checksums.
Manual: independent reviewer traces sampled claims to pseudonymous sessions and interview notes.
tasks.handoff_notes: GATE-009 combines this with the independent validity audit.
tasks.notes: Milestone-cap spend: 0.40 focused lead day allocated to S1.5.

tasks.context: Frozen Stage 1 question: does the technically valid, accessible and immutable Stage 1 build produce voluntary replay and improvement behavior strongly enough to justify Stage 2?
tasks.title: Audit technical validity and threshold integrity
tasks.summary: Independently classify external sessions and verify the formal round remains technically valid.
tasks.objective: Prevent reliability failures, post-hoc exclusions or threshold drift from distorting behavioral results.
inputs: Consumed contracts: Diagnostics/Run Validity v1 and BuildIdentity/ContentVersion v1; AR-007 matrix, frozen protocol and immutable external sessions.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: source sessions, protocol, behavioral analysis, implementation, candidate build and final decision.
Serialized ownership: none. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: none.
Rollback: remove derived audit and repeat from immutable sessions. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: Every session is classified through accepted rules with direct diagnostic and identity links.
More than 20 percent technically invalid sessions invalidates the round and prevents GATE-009 completion.
No post-hoc threshold, population, build or method change is present.
verification: Automated: recalculate invalid ratio, identity consistency, classification coverage and protocol checksum.
Manual: independent reviewer audits every exclusion/deviation and signs the result.
tasks.handoff_notes: An invalid round must be rerun/versioned; it cannot proceed through GATE-009.
tasks.notes: Milestone-cap spend: 0.30 focused lead day allocated to S1.5.

tasks.context: Frozen Stage 1 question: does the technically valid, accessible and immutable Stage 1 build produce voluntary replay and improvement behavior strongly enough to justify Stage 2?
tasks.title: Produce the Stage 1 outcome and pivot-or-stop report
tasks.summary: Combine valid behavioral evidence with technical, accessibility and capacity findings into one bounded recommendation.
tasks.objective: Recommend advance, versioned rerun, evidence-backed pivot or stop without authorizing implementation or Stage 2.
inputs: Consumed contracts: BuildIdentity/ContentVersion v1 and Diagnostics/Run Validity v1; behavioral analysis, validity audit, accepted failure policy and milestone-cap record requirements.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: implementation, tuning, source evidence, planning thresholds, task backlog, collaboration state and Stage 2 unlock.
Serialized ownership: none. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: none.
Rollback: remove derived reports and regenerate from immutable analyses. Prototype debt: none.
acceptance_criteria: Report states pass/fail for every frozen external signal and preserves technical/accessibility blockers.
A failure follows the accepted maximum-two-rounds then one-pivot policy and records cap impact, scope cuts and stop rationale.
Recommendation is one of advance, versioned rerun, bounded pivot or stop; it does not itself unlock Stage 2.
verification: Automated: validate report schema, source fingerprints, threshold outcomes, failure count and recommendation enum.
Manual: human lead reviews evidence, cap pressure and expand/stop rationale without editing source analyses.
tasks.handoff_notes: Only an advance recommendation may allow GATE-010 to complete.
tasks.notes: Milestone-cap spend: 0.40 focused lead day allocated to S1.5.

tasks.context: Frozen Stage 1 question: does the technically valid, accessible and immutable Stage 1 build produce voluntary replay and improvement behavior strongly enough to justify Stage 2?
tasks.title: Record the final Stage 1 approval and Stage 2 unlock
tasks.summary: Create the sole accepted Stage 1 approval record consumed by every Stage 2 implementation task.
tasks.objective: Unlock Stage 2 only when all technical, behavioral, accessibility and policy gates pass with immutable evidence.
inputs: Consumed contracts: BuildIdentity/ContentVersion v1 and Diagnostics/Run Validity v1; signed advance recommendation and complete immutable Stage 1 evidence chain.
implementation_notes: Forbidden paths: implementation, planning artifacts, source evidence, thresholds, task backlog, collaboration assignments and Stage 2 files.
Serialized ownership: none. Save-schema impact: none. Content-registry impact: none.
Rollback: revoke/remove unlock records and return all Stage 2 tasks to blocked. Prototype debt: none.
A reject, rerun, pivot or stop decision is recorded in GATE-009 but does not satisfy or complete this approval task.
acceptance_criteria: GATE-009 recommends advance and every linked technical, behavioral, accessibility, validity and cap criterion passes.
The human lead signs the exact evidence fingerprints and explicitly sets stage2_unlocked=true.
Any non-pass leaves this task blocked/rejected, so no Stage 2 dependency becomes ready.
verification: Automated: validate signature fields, evidence chain, advance recommendation, fingerprints and stage2_unlocked semantics.
Manual: human lead conducts the final go/no-go review and signs only after independently tracing required evidence.
tasks.handoff_notes: All Stage 2 implementation must depend on this exact approval task and additionally require stage2_unlocked=true.
tasks.notes: Milestone-cap spend: 0.20 focused lead day allocated to S1.5. External-round allocation totals 3.40 days, preserving 1.60 S1.5 days for invalid-session replacement, review and bounded follow-up.
